mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances when unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint high growth and inflation periods produce positive returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term equity momentum as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of easing cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales demonstrate strong consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth above 1.5% is followed by negative forward returns in more than 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, supporting higher corporate earnings and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A discrete increase in the federal funds rate signals active monetary tightening, which immediately increases discount rates and triggers negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of rate hike instances.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong construction activity and consumer confidence, serving as a leading indicator of economic expansion that supports positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, with a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.
